rag_rules: Preserve the chosen owner kind when merging: type-scope annex content stays on a component type, and implementation-scope content stays on a component implementation.
Merge annex text without changing existing architecture declarations, names, nesting, or end statements.
generic_rules: Insert the new annex as additive text; keep existing features, subcomponents, connections, and properties untouched.
Respect scope boundaries instead of relocating annex content to chase identifier visibility.
If merging into an implementation body, avoid disrupting the structural declaration flow needed by that implementation.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE text is wrapped as annex agree {** ... **}; inside a valid component or implementation body.
Owner scope matters: component-type contract content and implementation-level binding content are not interchangeable.
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite architectural structure or reorder declarations just to fit the annex.
Do not merge annex text into the wrong owner based on name similarity or referenced paths.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement-to-clause choice patterns such as range guarantees or dependency guarantees
AADL property names treated as AGREE expression variables
